text:
**BetSharper & MattHewP07

** Flemings over 25.5 PRAY
Ngongba over 6.5 rebounds
ocr_texts: Patrick Ngongba is the starting center for Duke, he gets a prime matchup to dominate the boards tonight against Syracuse. On the seaosn he averages 6.2 rebounds per game, but this includes any and all type of matchups on top of blowouts/foul trouble games. The key here is he should have a significantly better chance of staying out of foull trouble vs an inferior Syracuse team, the few games Patrick has gotten in foul trouble were against ranked teams like UNC and Louisville. Cuse gets just 18.5% of their pts from the FT line which is 261st in the country. Getting into his numbers in conference play, Ngongba has some clear splits performing much better on the boards against weak rebounding teams as opposed to the better reb teams. The games he covered this prop in conference play were against UNC (7), BC (9), Wake (7), Cal (7), FSU (7), Georgia Tech (9) These teams rank 135th,320th,214th,283rd, 344th, and 260th respectively. Thats great news considering Patrick gets another great matchup tonight vs Syracuse who allows 36.8 total rebounds game ranking 294th in the country. Of ACC teams, they are the 2nd worst rebounding team in the conference. Syracuse allows 10.2 offensive rebs/game ranking 319th, notably Ngongba gets 37%of his rebs on the offensive end. We see the games Ngongba fell short of this prop in ACC play, they were vs Clemson, VT, Louisville twice, Stanford,SMU. These teams rank 41st,224th, 101st, 70th, and 139th. Most of these being much better rebounding teams than Syracuse. This game is at home, Duke's 2nd best defensive efficiency in the nation should limit their scoring allowing for ample rebounding opportunities. Duke holds teams to 37.7 FG% at home, Cuse shoots alot worse on road.

Kingston Flemings gets a big primetime top 5 matchup tonight against the Cyclones. This is a big conference game and should be very competitive with a close 2.5 point spread. The stud Freshman, projected top 5 NBA draft pick, has been having an excellent season this far and he's stepped up most in the biggest games. Overall we see Kingston is averaging 16.4 pts, 3.6 rebounds and 5.3 assists in 31.1 minutes per game on efficient shooting splits of 49.2% FG's, 36.5% on 3's and 84% on FT's. However this includes a bunch of games against weaker competition and/or blowout where Flemings wasn't necessarily needed to carry a huge load in terms of usage or minutes. When we look at his 6 games played against ranked opponents, that's when he rises to the moment most. Against ranked teams, Kingston is averaging 34.83 PRA per game. He's had 29+ PRA in each of those and most encouraging is the huge volume in those games. Flemings is averaging 23.33 FG+FT attempts per game, this is against the likes of Texas Texh twice, BYU, Arkansas, Tennessee and Auburn. Another encouraging sign is that a fact he averaged over 35 mins in these games. On the road specifically in conference play with 30+ mins, he has put up 29, 37, 50, and 28 PRA (29 & 50 vs the ranked opponents). While this against a good defense in lowa St, we have seen athletic higher USG guards have success against them: Cam Carr 33 PRA @ ISU, Jaylen Curry 26 PRA, Melvin Council 24 PRA, PJ Haggerty 35 PRA. These have just been recently in the huge usage and mins he should get. Big stage game, l expect the potential top 5pick tolook to put on a show here.

text: **PorterPicks**
ocr_texts: HOUSTON (+2.5) over lowa State (4-UNITS)苗 6:00pm PST - ESPW Free throw percentage will be a big part of the outcome of this game as lowa State struggles from the free throw line not even being ranked in the top-300 of the country! Houston is a solid free three shooting team being ranked top-25 in the country. They could easily be undefeated if it wasn't for a one basket loss to Tennessee on a neutral court and to Texas Tech by four points. Keep in mind, lowa State has losses to Cincy and TCU and they did get manhandled by Kansas as well earlier this season. Sure, they got their revenge against Kansas on Saturday. however, that was their most toughest opponent they've faced at home this season and they caught Kansas in a let-down spot after defeating Arizona in their prior game. This is the same lowa State team that barely beat Baylor by 3 points at home and they barely beat a bad lowa team at home by only 4 points earlier in the year. Remember, Houston has played a more tougher schedule this season and they are a top-10
